steps: Log in to your Freshdesk account at https://yoursubdomain.freshdesk.com
Click your profile picture or avatar in the top-right corner
Select 'Profile settings' from the dropdown menu
On the right-hand pane, click 'View API key'
Complete the CAPTCHA verification if prompted
Use the key with HTTP Basic Auth: set the username to the API key and the password to any string (e.g., 'X')
Encode username:password in Base64 and set the Authorization header to 'Basic <encoded_value>'

credential_fields.name: api_key
credential_fields.label: API Key
credential_fields.description: Your Freshdesk API key, found in Profile Settings. Used as the HTTP Basic Auth username with any password.

notes: Freshdesk uses HTTP Basic Auth: set the username to the API key and password to 'X' (or any non-empty string)
The base URL is https://{yoursubdomain}.freshdesk.com — you must use your own subdomain
Resetting your API key will disconnect all apps currently using it
Freshdesk offers a free plan (Sprout) for up to 10 agents with limited features
Rate limits vary by plan: Free plan is 100 requests/minute, higher plans allow more
API key is tied to the agent account — activities via API are governed by the agent's role

test_endpoint.description: List the most recent support tickets. Requires your subdomain in the base URL.
